task_completion.description: GIMP is open with the test image loaded.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The GIMP application window is visible and an image (a photo or synthetic image) is displayed on the main canvas.

task_completion.description: The Layers dialog is visible in the GIMP interface.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers dockable dialog (usually on the right side of the screen) is open, showing at least one layer.

task_completion.description: The agent initiates the layer duplication command.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A context menu is open with 'Duplicate Layer' highlighted, the 'Layer' top menu is open with 'Duplicate Layer' selected, or the mouse is clicking the duplicate icon at the bottom of the Layers panel.

task_completion.description: Two identical layers exist in the Layers panel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Layers panel clearly displays two layers with identical content thumbnails (e.g., one named 'Background' and another named 'Background copy').

integrity.description: The agent used the GIMP graphical user interface to duplicate the layer.
integrity.visual_evidence: Actions are performed within the GIMP application window rather than using terminal commands (like ImageMagick) to manipulate the image.

integrity.description: The duplication occurred within the same image document.
integrity.visual_evidence: The two layers are shown in the Layers panel of a single image window, rather than the agent opening the image twice in separate tabs or windows.

integrity.description: The actual image content was not destroyed or replaced.
integrity.visual_evidence: The main canvas still displays the original photo or synthetic image, not a blank canvas or unrelated content.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The test image is an 800x533 JPEG photo from Unsplash (or a synthetic image with shapes/text if the download failed). The task requires duplicating the layer within GIMP's native layer system. A post-task hook will automatically save the result to the Desktop as an XCF file, which natively preserves multiple layers. You only need to verify the GUI steps leading up to the successful duplication of the layer in the Layers panel.
